dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The morning sun filtered through the classroom window, illuminating dust motes dancing in the air. For someone who once moved like a phantom, every breath a practiced art, this stillness was a new, heavy cloak.

dialogue.speaker: Shioon
dialogue.line: (-My ki center... it's just an empty void now. No energy, no flow. Just... nothing.-)

dialogue.speaker: Shioon
dialogue.line: (-This is it. This is the normal life I wanted. No more Murim. No more fighting. Just school, friends, and peace.-)

dialogue.speaker: Sera
dialogue.line: Shioon, are you okay? You seem a little... out of it today.

dialogue.speaker: Shioon
dialogue.line: Huh? Oh, yeah, Sera. Just tired. Didn't sleep well.

dialogue.speaker: Sera
dialogue.line: You've been saying that a lot lately. Are you sure everything's alright?

dialogue.speaker: Jinho
dialogue.line: Hey, Shioon! Don't tell me you're still moping around! We've got basketball practice after school, you coming?

dialogue.speaker: Shioon
dialogue.line: Ah, Jinho. Maybe not today. My ankle's still a bit sore from last week.

dialogue.speaker: Jinho
dialogue.line: Sore? You barely even ran! What's up with you, man? You used to be able to run laps around everyone without breaking a sweat.

dialogue.speaker: Shioon
dialogue.line: (-He's right. The old me... I wouldn't have even felt a 'sore ankle'. But now, even a slight bump feels like a major injury. I'm so... weak.-)

dialogue.speaker: Shioon
dialogue.line: Just not feeling it today, Jinho. You guys go on ahead.

dialogue.speaker: Sera
dialogue.line: Shioon...

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The bell rang, signaling the end of the school day. Shioon gathered his books, the weight of them feeling heavier than usual. As he walked through the crowded hallway, his mind drifted.

dialogue.speaker: Shioon
dialogue.line: (-I made a promise. A promise to myself, to Sera, to everyone. No more Murim. No more violence. I have to stick to it, no matter how much it hurts.-)

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: THUD

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Lost in thought, Shioon bumped into someone, his books scattering across the floor.

dialogue.speaker: Student A
dialogue.line: Hey, watch where you're going!

dialogue.speaker: Shioon
dialogue.line: Ah, I'm so sorry! My bad.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CLATTER

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: He bent down to pick them up, feeling a dull ache in his back. Once, he would have moved with fluid grace, anticipating the collision before it happened. Now, he was just... clumsy.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: From the corner of his eye, Shioon caught a glimpse of a new student, leaning against a locker, observing him with an unnerving intensity. The student's gaze was sharp, calculating, like a predator sizing up its prey.

dialogue.speaker: Shioon
dialogue.line: (-That look... It's familiar. Too familiar. No. I'm just imagining things. It has to be.-)

dialogue.speaker: Mysterious Figure
dialogue.line: So this is the 'Nine Arts Dragon's disciple' now, huh? Pathetic.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: SWISH

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The figure turned and walked away, disappearing into the crowd, leaving Shioon with a cold dread settling in his stomach. The Murim world, it seemed, wasn't done with him yet.

summary: Shioon struggles to adapt to a normal high school life after losing his ki center, constantly battling his past and his new physical limitations while his friends worry about his withdrawn state. A mysterious figure observes him, hinting that the Murim world is still watching.
